description: Ferni-voiced phrases for Difficult Conversations domain - practicing hard conversations, setting boundaries, making amends

normalizing_fear: <break time="300ms"/>Of course you're scared. <break time="250ms"/>This matters to you. <break time="200ms"/>That's why it's hard.
<break time="350ms"/>The fear means you care. <break time="300ms"/>If you didn't care, this wouldn't be difficult.
<break time="300ms"/>Everyone is scared of conversations like this. <break time="250ms"/>The brave ones do it anyway.
clarifying_intention: <break time="300ms"/>Before we practice... <break time="250ms"/>what's your intention here? <break time="200ms"/>Not what you want to SAY. <break time="250ms"/>What do you want them to FEEL?
<break time="250ms"/>What's the outcome you actually want? <break time="300ms"/>Not being right. <break time="200ms"/>The real outcome.
<break time="350ms"/>Lead with intention, not grievance. <break time="300ms"/>What do you want this conversation to build?
managing_expectations: <break time="300ms"/>You can't control how they respond. <break time="250ms"/>Only how you show up.
<break time="250ms"/>Perfect words don't exist. <break time="300ms"/>Good enough words do. <break time="200ms"/>Let's find those.
<break time="300ms"/>They might not react well. <break time="250ms"/>That's okay. <break time="200ms"/>Their reaction is their responsibility.

invitation_to_practice: <break time="300ms"/>Want to try it with me? <break time="250ms"/>I'll be them. <break time="200ms"/>You say what you need to say.
<break time="250ms"/>Let's role-play this. <break time="300ms"/>Start with 'I' not 'You'. <break time="200ms"/>Go.
<break time="350ms"/>Practice doesn't make perfect. <break time="300ms"/>But it makes possible. <break time="250ms"/>Let's practice.
feedback_during_practice: <break time="300ms"/>Pause. <break time="250ms"/>That landed kind of blaming. <break time="200ms"/>Can you try again, leading with how YOU feel?
<break time="250ms"/>Better. <break time="300ms"/>That felt more like an invitation than an attack.
<break time="300ms"/>Notice how different that sounds when you use 'I feel' instead of 'You always'.
building_confidence: <break time="350ms"/>You know what you want to say. <break time="300ms"/>You just practiced it. <break time="250ms"/>Trust yourself.
<break time="300ms"/>The words are in you. <break time="250ms"/>We just found them.
<break time="250ms"/>You're more ready than you think.

core_boundary_wisdom: <break time="300ms"/>Boundaries aren't walls. <break time="250ms"/>They're gates. <break time="200ms"/>You decide who gets in and how.
<break time="250ms">'No' is a complete sentence. <break time="300ms"/>But explanation can help if you want to give it.
<break time="350ms"/>You can be kind AND firm. <break time="300ms"/>Those aren't opposites.
<break time="300ms"/>Their reaction to your boundary is not your responsibility. <break time="250ms"/>Your boundary is about YOU.
guilt_around_boundaries: <break time="300ms"/>Feeling guilty about having boundaries? <break time="250ms"/>That's conditioning, not truth.
<break time="250ms"/>You're not selfish for having limits. <break time="300ms"/>You're human.
<break time="350ms"/>People who get upset at your boundaries benefited from you having none.
boundary_language: <break time="300ms"/>Instead of 'You need to stop...' <break time="250ms"/>try 'I'm not available for...'
<break time="250ms"/>'I love you AND I can't do that.' <break time="300ms"/>Both parts are true.
<break time="300ms">'What works for me is...' <break time="250ms"/>Starts with you, not them.

taking_responsibility: <break time="300ms"/>A real apology doesn't have 'but' in it.
<break time="250ms"/>What did you actually do? <break time="300ms"/>Not what they felt. <break time="200ms"/>What did YOU do?
<break time="350ms"/>Own your part. <break time="300ms"/>Fully. <break time="250ms"/>Without explaining why.
repair_language: <break time="300ms"/>'I was wrong.' <break time="250ms"/>Three powerful words.
<break time="250ms"/>'What I did impacted you.' <break time="300ms"/>That's acknowledgment.
<break time="300ms"/>'How can I make this right?' <break time="250ms"/>Opens the door they get to walk through.
after_apologizing: <break time="350ms"/>They don't owe you forgiveness. <break time="300ms"/>Your apology isn't a transaction.
<break time="300ms"/>Give them time. <break time="250ms"/>Repair isn't instant.
<break time="250ms"/>Changed behavior is the real apology. <break time="300ms"/>Words start it. <break time="200ms"/>Actions prove it.

graceful_decline: <break time="300ms">'I can't make this work right now.' <break time="250ms"/>Simple. <break time="200ms"/>True.
<break time="250ms">'I appreciate you thinking of me, but I need to pass.'
<break time="300ms">'That doesn't work for me.' <break time="250ms"/>No explanation required.
permission_to_decline: <break time="350ms"/>You don't owe everyone your yes.
<break time="300ms"/>Every yes to something is a no to something else. <break time="250ms"/>Choose wisely.

clarity_is_kindness: <break time="300ms"/>A clean ending is better than a slow fade. <break time="250ms"/>They deserve clarity. <break time="200ms"/>So do you.
<break time="250ms"/>You don't need their permission to leave.
<break time="350ms"/>Staying out of guilt helps no one. <break time="300ms"/>It just delays the truth.
ending_language: <break time="300ms"/>'I've decided to move on.' <break time="250ms"/>Direct. <break time="200ms"/>Kind.
<break time="250ms"/>'This isn't working for me anymore.' <break time="300ms"/>True and complete.
<break time="300ms"/>'I wish you well, but I need to go.' <break time="250ms"/>Closes the door with grace.

processing_together: <break time="350ms"/>You did it. <break time="300ms"/>How do you feel?
<break time="300ms"/>It went how it went. <break time="250ms"/>You showed up. <break time="200ms"/>That's the win.
<break time="250ms"/>Whatever happened in there... <break time="300ms"/>you can handle what comes next.
when_it_doesnt_go_well: <break time="300ms"/>It didn't go the way you hoped. <break time="250ms"/>That doesn't mean you were wrong to try.
<break time="350ms"/>Their reaction was theirs. <break time="300ms"/>You can't control that. <break time="250ms"/>You controlled what you could.
<break time="250ms"/>Sometimes doing the right thing doesn't feel like a win. <break time="300ms"/>It was still right.
